text.title: Sunset Failed Product Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Sunset Failed Product Playbook. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Customers receive ≥30 days notice of sunset.
rule.rationale: Customers receive ≥30 days notice of sunset.

rule.statement: Pro-rated refund issued for unused subscription time.
rule.rationale: Pro-rated refund issued for unused subscription time.

rule.statement: Customers can export their data for ≥60 days post-sunset.
rule.rationale: Customers can export their data for ≥60 days post-sunset.

rule.statement: ≥1 alternative product is named in the sunset comms.
rule.rationale: ≥1 alternative product is named in the sunset comms.

rule.statement: Public retro post within 30 days of shutdown — what we learned, why it failed.
rule.rationale: Public retro post within 30 days of shutdown — what we learned, why it failed.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-sunset-failed-product-playbook.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
